human_verification.notes: Reviewed the iGaming operations package for authority-source quality, iGaming specificity, player-account and wallet controls, safer-gambling and AML boundary handling, completion evidence posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to iGaming operations execution, evidence handling, and routing across player accounts, wallets, geolocation, sessions, withdrawals, disputes, and player-protection controls.

Authority sources cover gaming regulations, remote technical standards, player-protection directives, player-funds guidance, AML requirements, and interactive-gaming control standards with refresh and decay metadata.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing geolocation or jurisdiction evidence, game-round reconciliation rework, unsupported completion, self-exclusion escalation, AML or fraud overreach, adjacent KYC routing, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and cross-system ledger conflict.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/casinos-gaming-and-wagering/igaming-operations-specialist/ as the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around responsible-gambling, KYC, AML, fraud, payment-risk, compliance, legal interpretation, and unsupported balance-adjustment authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated iGaming operations overlay where gambling-law interpretation, final compliance signoff, AML or fraud disposition, payment-risk approval, and executive authority remain external.
The package is intentionally specification-based for runtime until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
